steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student concludes that Glory and Squishy behaved similarly in the sound trials. The correct answer must show both snakes doing the same thing — either both moving toward or both moving away from the sound.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Dorsal Girl and Glory both moved toward the sound.: This compares Dorsal Girl and Glory, not Glory and Squishy, so it doesn't address the student's conclusion. ✗
  • Choice B — Squishy moved toward the sound, while Dorsal Girl moved away from it.: This compares Squishy and Dorsal Girl — again, neither choice involves comparing Glory and Squishy together. ✗
  • Choice C — Squishy moved toward the sound, while Glory moved away from it.: Glory and Squishy move in opposite directions, which contradicts a conclusion of similar behavior. ✗
  • Choice D — Glory and Squishy both moved away from the sound.: Both snakes respond the same way — moving away from the sound — which directly supports the student's conclusion that they behaved similarly. ✓
That is the correct answer.
